pattern: Binary search compares the midpoint with the right end to decide which half still contains the rotation point, the location of the minimum.
hints: The minimum is the single place where a larger value is immediately followed by a smaller one; everything else is increasing.
Compare the middle element with the rightmost: if the middle is larger, the drop lies to its right, otherwise it is at the middle or to its left.
Keep a [lo, hi] window: when nums[mid] > nums[hi] set lo = mid + 1, else hi = mid; when lo meets hi that index holds the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: rotated so minimum sits in the middle
fully rotated back to sorted, minimum at front
minimum at the very last position

stress.description: large array of distinct sorted values rotated by a random amount
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    vals = rng.sample(range(-5000, 5001), n)
    vals.sort()
    k = rng.randrange(1, n + 1)
    rotated = vals[k % n:] + vals[:k % n]
    return [rotated]
stress.note: distinct values sorted then rotated, satisfying the rotation guarantee
